bylaws.title: Off Road Vehicle & All-terrain Vehicles
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw allows ATVs and off-road vehicles with 3+ wheels to be driven on Township roads. Drivers must follow provincial Highway Traffic Act rules.
ai_key_points: ATVs and off-road vehicles with 3+ wheels can drive on Township roads
Vehicles must have low-pressure tires (70 kpa/10 psi or less)
Must follow all Highway Traffic Act rules and regulations
Penalties follow Highway Traffic Act - fines and charges apply
Only applies to roads controlled by the Township

bylaws.title: Sale of Land Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets the rules for how Nipissing Township can sell municipal land. It requires public notice, appraisals, and council approval before any land sales.
ai_key_points: Council must declare land 'surplus' before selling it
Public notice is required before selling any municipal land
Professional appraisals are required unless land fits specific exemptions
Council can choose sale method: public tender, real estate agent, or direct sale
Some small parcels and transfers to other governments don't need appraisals

bylaws.title: Trailer Use (Temporary during Construction)
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates rules for using trailers as temporary homes while building a permanent house in Nipissing Township.
ai_key_points: You can live in a trailer temporarily while constructing your permanent home
Must follow specific requirements outlined in the township's policy
Policy details and application process are in attached schedules
By-law passed in September 2011 to formalize these rules

bylaws.title: Entrance 911 By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw requires property owners to display municipal address numbers on clearly visible signs for 911 emergency services. The Township installs the first sign but owners pay for it and must maintain it.
ai_key_points: All properties with entrances or buildings must display Township-assigned address numbers
Signs must be clearly visible from the road using specific materials and design
Property owners pay for the initial sign through their building/entrance permit fees
Owners must maintain signs in good condition or face replacement costs
Unpaid sign costs get added to your property tax bill after 30 days

bylaws.title: Authorize Speed Limit Changes
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets a 60 km/h speed limit on all township roads that don't already have posted speed limit signs.
ai_key_points: All unposted roads in Nipissing Township now have a 60 km/h speed limit
Only applies to roads without existing speed limit signs
Standard Highway Traffic Act penalties apply for speeding violations
Public meeting was held in November 2013 before passing this rule

bylaws.title: Amend BL 1088 – Off Road Vehicles on Municipal Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows off-road vehicles like ATVs to drive on Township of Nipissing municipal roads with specific speed limits and equipment requirements.
ai_key_points: Off-road vehicles can drive on municipal roads in Township of Nipissing
Speed limit is 20 km/h on roads with 50 km/h limit, 50 km/h on faster roads
Vehicles must meet provincial equipment requirements under Ontario regulations
Cannot drive on community centres, parks, beaches, or cemeteries except parking areas
Breaking these rules is an offence with legal penalties

bylaws.title: Minimum Maintenance Standards – Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: Adopts minimum maintenance standards for municipal roads and highways as required by Ontario regulation. Defines plowing timelines, pothole repair schedules, and road surface conditions the township must meet.
ai_key_points: Sets snow clearing timelines after storms
Defines acceptable road surface conditions
Pothole repair timelines by road classification
Protects township from liability when standards are met
Based on Ontario Regulation 366/18

bylaws.title: Amending Maximum Weight on Bridges
bylaws.ai_summary: Amends the maximum weight limits permitted on township bridges based on engineering assessments. Restricts heavy vehicles from crossing bridges that cannot safely support their weight.
ai_key_points: Weight limits posted at each affected bridge
Based on structural engineering assessments
Heavy vehicles must use alternate routes
Fines for exceeding posted weight limits
Protects bridges from structural damage

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Powassan
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates an agreement with the Town of Powassan about maintaining and managing a boundary road that runs between the two municipalities.
ai_key_points: Establishes shared responsibility for a road on the border with Powassan
Sets out maintenance duties and costs for the boundary road
Defines which municipality handles what aspects of road management

bylaws.title: Public Dock By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw regulates how residents can use Township public docks at Wades Landing, Chapman's Landing, and McQuaby Lake Launch for loading and unloading only.
ai_key_points: Boats can only park at docks for 4 hours maximum during daytime (7am-11pm)
Materials can only be stored on docks for 1 hour while loading/unloading
No overnight parking of boats or storing boats on Township land
Commercial boats need Township permission before using public docks
Fines range from $150-$400 for violations like overstaying or blocking access

bylaws.title: Landfill By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Governs the use of the township landfill site, including hours of operation, accepted materials, and tipping fees. Residents must show proof of residency to access the facility.
ai_key_points: Proof of residency required for landfill access
Hazardous waste and certain materials prohibited
Tipping fees apply to construction debris and large loads
Seasonal hours of operation posted at the site
Illegal dumping subject to fines and cleanup costs

bylaws.title: Amending Minimum Maintenance Standards Schedule E
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township updated their snow plow route schedule to remove specific truck assignments from routes. This gives them more flexibility to respond to winter storms.
ai_key_points: Updates the Township's snow plow route assignments from 2019
Removes specific truck numbers assigned to particular plow routes
Allows more flexible response to winter weather events
Changes took effect December 15, 2020

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates a Recreation Committee to run recreational programs and manage the Township Community Centre at 2381 Highway 654.
ai_key_points: Committee has 2 council members plus 4-8 residents appointed yearly
Committee manages Community Centre and fitness centre operations
Committee sets user fees for facilities (subject to council approval)
Committee must submit annual budget to council by February 1st
Committee must hold at least 4 meetings per year

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: Township creates a Cemetery Committee to manage three local cemeteries. Committee has 5 members and must follow provincial cemetery rules.
ai_key_points: Committee manages Nipissing Union, Commanda, and St. John's Alsace cemeteries
5 members total: 2 council members and 3 township residents
Committee creates cemetery rules following provincial laws
Must submit annual budget to council by February 1st
Must hold at least one meeting per year

bylaws.title: Community Safety Zone
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township created a Community Safety Zone on streets around South Shore Education Centre with a 40 km/h speed limit and doubled speeding fines.
ai_key_points: Community Safety Zone covers Beatty Street, Front Street, Blake Street, and part of Powerplant Road
Speed limit is 40 km/h at all times, every day of the year
Speeding fines are doubled in this zone
Zone protects elementary school, park, and ice rink from truck traffic
Ontario Provincial Police enforce the rules

bylaws.title: Parking By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes parking regulations on township roads and public areas. Restricts overnight parking during winter months to allow for snow clearing operations and sets time limits in designated areas.
ai_key_points: No overnight parking on roads from November to April
Parking restrictions support winter snow removal
Time-limited parking in designated public areas
Vehicles in violation may be towed at owner expense
Fines set for each parking infraction

bylaws.title: Unopened Road Allowances By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law regulates how people can use unopened road allowances (unused public land designated for roads). It sets rules for access and activities on these properties.
ai_key_points: Prohibits unauthorized use of unopened road allowances without municipal permission
Requires permits or agreements before using these lands for any purpose
Protects public access rights to these designated road areas
Establishes fines and enforcement procedures for violations

bylaws.title: Unauthorized Disposal of Waste
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law prohibits illegal dumping of garbage, recycling, or other waste on public or private property in Nipissing Township without permission.
ai_key_points: Cannot dump any waste on roads, parks, or other people's property
Applies to all types of garbage, recycling, and hazardous materials
Violators can be fined and must clean up the mess
Property owners must prevent dumping on their land

bylaws.title: Depositing of Snow on any Highway Or Bridge
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law prohibits residents from dumping or pushing snow onto roads and bridges in Nipissing Township. Violations can result in fines.
ai_key_points: No dumping or depositing snow onto any road or bridge
No pushing snow from driveways or properties onto public roads
Applies to all highways and bridges in the township
Violators can be fined under provincial offences laws

bylaws.title: Amend Zoning By-Law 2020-20 Section 3.44 Trailers
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law changes the rules about trailers in Nipissing Township. It updates an existing zoning law from 2020 that controls where and how trailers can be used.
ai_key_points: Updates existing trailer regulations in the township's zoning by-law
Modifies Section 3.44 which specifically deals with trailer rules
Changes to zoning requirements that affect trailer placement and use
Amends the 2020 zoning by-law with new trailer provisions

bylaws.title: Culvert/Entrance
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw sets rules for installing culverts and driveways that connect to Township roads. You need a permit and must follow specific installation standards.
ai_key_points: You must get Township approval before installing any driveway entrance to a municipal road
Culverts must be new, minimum 400mm diameter, 9 meters long, made of approved materials
Property owners pay all costs for installation, maintenance, and any road damage they cause
Only one entrance per home is allowed; work must start within 6 months
Unpaid repair bills get added to your property taxes after 30 days

bylaws.title: Procedural By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes rules and procedures for council meetings, including how agendas are set, how motions are made, public participation rules, and rules of order for elected officials.
ai_key_points: Sets regular meeting schedule for council
Defines rules for public delegations and deputations
Outlines how motions and amendments are handled
Establishes quorum requirements
Governs closed session procedures

bylaws.title: Cemetery Regulations
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for Township of Nipissing cemeteries, covering burial procedures, grave maintenance, monument standards, and visitor conduct.
ai_key_points: Cemetery operations including burials, disinterments, and grave preparations must follow township procedures
Monuments and grave markers must meet specific size, material, and installation requirements
Cemetery grounds have rules for decorations, flowers, and maintenance of grave sites
Visitors must follow conduct rules and cemetery hours of operation
Violations can result in fines and enforcement action by township officials

bylaws.title: Dog By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Regulates dog ownership in Nipissing Township, including licensing requirements, leash rules, and noise provisions. Establishes penalties for owners of dogs that cause disturbances or pose a threat to public safety.
ai_key_points: All dogs must be licensed annually with the township
Dogs must be leashed when off owner property
Owners liable for damage caused by their dog
Maximum number of dogs per household may apply
Fines for barking complaints and at-large violations

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Callander
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates an agreement between Nipissing Township and Callander about maintaining and managing a shared boundary road between the two municipalities.
ai_key_points: Establishes formal agreement for shared responsibility of boundary road with Callander
Defines maintenance duties and costs for the shared roadway
Sets out which municipality handles what road services and repairs

bylaws.title: Accountability & Transparency
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for how Township of Nipissing officials must be open and accountable to residents. It requires transparency in government operations and decision-making processes.
ai_key_points: Township officials must be transparent and accountable in their duties to residents
Government operations and decision-making processes must be conducted openly
Sets standards for public access to municipal information and proceedings
Establishes requirements for reporting and disclosure by township officials

bylaws.title: Delegation of Authority
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows Township Council to give certain decision-making powers to staff members like the CAO and department heads for day-to-day operations.
ai_key_points: Council can delegate specific powers and duties to township staff members
Staff can make certain decisions without needing full council approval
Delegation helps streamline township operations and administrative processes
Council retains oversight and can revoke delegated authority if needed

bylaws.title: Regulate Sale of Land
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for selling land in Nipissing Township. It requires permits and following specific procedures when selling property.
ai_key_points: Property owners must get a permit before selling land
Sales must follow township procedures and requirements
Violations can result in fines or other penalties
The by-law protects orderly land development in the township

bylaws.title: Nuisance Beaver By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Allows property owners to trap or remove nuisance beavers that are causing flooding or property damage, subject to notification to the township and compliance with provincial wildlife regulations.
ai_key_points: Property owners may remove beavers causing damage
Township must be notified before removal
Must comply with provincial trapping regulations
Beaver dam removal permitted to prevent flooding
Township may assist with removal in some cases

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Recreation Committee for the Township of Nipissing to oversee recreational programs and facilities in the community.
ai_key_points: Creates a Recreation Committee to manage township recreational activities and facilities
Sets out committee membership, meeting requirements, and operational procedures
Defines the committee's responsibilities for recreation planning and recommendations

bylaws.title: Museum Board
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Museum Board for the Township of Nipissing to oversee and manage the local museum operations and governance.
ai_key_points: Creates a Museum Board to manage the township's museum
Sets rules for board membership and appointments
Defines the board's powers and responsibilities
Establishes meeting requirements and procedures

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Cemetery Committee for the Township of Nipissing to oversee and manage local cemeteries and burial grounds.
ai_key_points: Creates a Cemetery Committee to manage township cemeteries
Committee oversees cemetery operations and maintenance
Establishes governance structure for burial ground management

bylaws.title: Establishing and Regulating Fire Department
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw officially establishes the Township of Nipissing Fire Department and sets rules for how it operates, including staff duties and cost recovery.
ai_key_points: Creates the Township of Nipissing Fire Department with a Fire Chief as head
New firefighters must complete 1-year probation and required training before becoming active
Fire Chief can discipline, suspend, or recommend firing firefighters for rule violations
Property owners may be charged for emergency response costs and special equipment
Fire department provides fire suppression, prevention, education, rescue and emergency services

bylaws.title: Social Media Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for how Township employees and officials use social media when representing the municipality or discussing Township business online.
ai_key_points: Township staff must follow professional standards when posting about work on social media
Employees cannot share confidential information or speak for the Township without permission
Personal social media use should not embarrass or harm the Township's reputation
Violations may result in disciplinary action including possible termination

bylaws.title: Code of Conduct
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes a code of conduct for members of council and local boards, setting ethical standards for behaviour, conflicts of interest, and use of public resources.
ai_key_points: Council members must declare conflicts of interest
Prohibits use of office for personal benefit
Sets standards for respectful conduct
Complaints handled by the Integrity Commissioner
Penalties range from reprimand to suspension of pay

bylaws.title: Trailer Licensing By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Requires licensing for trailers used as temporary or seasonal dwellings on private property. Sets conditions for trailer placement including lot size, servicing, and duration limits.
ai_key_points: Trailers on private lots require a township license
Annual license must be renewed
Minimum lot and setback requirements apply
Trailer must meet basic health and safety standards
License can be revoked for non-compliance

bylaws.title: provide for an Interim Tax Levy
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing sets interim property taxes for 2024. Property owners must pay these taxes while the municipality finalizes the full annual tax rate.
ai_key_points: Property owners must pay interim taxes for 2024 until final rates are set
Interim tax amounts are based on previous year's assessments and rates
Late payments may result in penalties and interest charges
Taxes help fund municipal services like roads, water, and emergency services

bylaws.title: appoint an Emergency Management Program Committee for the Township of Nipissing
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by T. Butler
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing created an Emergency Management Program Committee in 2024 to help plan for and respond to local emergencies and disasters.
ai_key_points: Creates a new committee to handle emergency planning and response for the township
Committee will develop plans for disasters like floods, fires, or other emergencies
Helps coordinate emergency services and community safety during crisis situations
